comments.author: @rmmmizan6817
comments.text:
مَنِ الصادقُ ومَنِ الزائف؟
إسلامُ اللهِ ورسولِه والقرآن—
لا يكادُ يُرى اليومَ في أيِّ مكان.
بل انتشرَ في أرجاءِ العالمِ
إسلامٌ صاغتهُ المصالحُ والربا.
فقد طارَ طائرُ السلامِ بعيدًا،
إلى سماءٍ مجهولةٍ وراءَ الآفاق.
وامتلأتِ المدنُ والحواضرُ والموانئ
بالظلمِ والجورِ والاضطراب.
لا تسيرُ المجتمعاتُ اليومَ
على حكمِ اللهِ وشريعته،
بل تسيرُ على قوانينَ وضعَها البشر.
يصلّون، ويصومون، ويحجّون، ويؤدّون الزكاة،
ولكنَّ الحكمَ والقضاءَ
يبقيانَ وفقَ قوانينِ الإنسان.
يتجاهلونَ ما نهى اللهُ عنه،
فأصبحَ العالمُ يسيرُ اليومَ
وفقَ أحكامِ البشر.
إنَّ حفظَ القرآنِ مسؤوليةٌ تولّاها اللهُ بنفسِه،
ولذلك بقيَ القرآنُ محفوظًا من التغيير.
وبعدَ ثلاثةَ عشرَ قرنًا من نزولِ القرآن،
جاءَ حزبُ التوحيد ليُبرزَ
المطالبَ الأساسيةَ للقرآن،
مستندًا إلى القرآنِ نفسِه.
ولهذا فإنَّ تجّارَ الدين
يشعرونَ بالغضبِ والانزعاج،
وقد أصبحوا يسعونَ بكلِّ قوة
لمواجهةِ حزبِ التوحيد.
إنَّ اللهَ مع الحقِّ دائمًا—
وهذا الإيمانُ ثابتٌ لا يتغيّر.
وسيتّضحُ قريبًا:
مَنِ الصادقُ،
ومَنِ الزائف.
ملاحظة:
هذه الأغنية لا تحمل أيَّ حقوقِ نشر. يمكن لأيِّ شخصٍ أن يضعَ لها لحنًا من اختياره، وأن يغنّيها أو يؤدّيها أو يسجّلها أو ينشرها دون الحاجةِ إلى إذن.

comments.author: @rmmmizan6817
comments.text:
Who Is True and Who Is False?
Allah, His Messenger, and the Qur'an's Islam—
Nowhere on earth are they truly found.
Across the world there spreads instead
an Islam shaped by interest and worldly gain.
So the bird of peace has flown away
beyond the far and unknown sky.
Injustice, oppression, and endless unrest
have filled the cities, towns, and harbors of the world.
Society no longer lives by Allah's law;
human laws now rule the courts.
People pray, they fast, they give and make the Hajj,
yet judgment follows man-made laws.
Ignoring what Allah has forbidden,
the world now walks by human rule.
The protection of the Qur'an belongs to Allah alone;
therefore, it remains preserved and unchanged.
Thirteen centuries after the Qur'an was revealed,
Hezbut Tawheed has brought forward
the Qur'an's original and central call,
drawn directly from the Qur'an itself.
That is why the merchants of religion
burn with anger in their hearts,
and have become relentless
in their opposition to Hezbut Tawheed.
Allah stands with the truth—
This faith remains unshaken.
Very soon the day will show
who is true, and who is false.
Note: This song is copyright-free. Anyone is welcome to compose a melody, sing, perform, record, or share it without seeking permission.

comments.author: @rmmmizan6817
comments.text:
Symbols, Ideals, and Reality
The phrase "La ilaha illallah" means "There is no lawgiver except Allah." Yet we have been taught that it means "There is no deity worthy of worship except Allah." In my view, this interpretation is incorrect. I believe that this misunderstanding has been one of the major causes of the long decline of the Muslim world.
The Saudi royal family has inscribed "La ilaha illallah" on its national flag, which, in my understanding, means: "We acknowledge no lawgiver except Allah." However, in my view, the policies and actions of the Saudi state are not consistent with that declaration. Rather than working to unite the Muslim world upon the social principles of the Qur'an, it has, in my opinion, given greater priority to political power, material prosperity, and luxury. For its national security, it has chosen to rely on security agreements with the United States.
Makkah and Madinah are the sacred pilgrimage sites of the entire Muslim Ummah, and they are located in Saudi Arabia. In my view, these holy cities should be regarded as a shared trust and spiritual heritage of the whole Muslim world. I therefore ask: despite the Kingdom's immense wealth, why should pilgrims be required to pay visa fees? At the same time, Saudi Arabia's role in regional conflicts, including the war in Yemen, has also raised serious questions and criticism.
Based on my understanding of the Qur'an, when individuals or governments set aside Allah's guidance and give priority to human-made laws, a fundamental contradiction arises between what they profess and how they govern. I therefore believe that the future of the Muslim world depends upon restoring the Qur'an's principles of justice, truth, and social order in public life—not merely proclaiming them through symbols or slogans.
